challenges.title: Responsive Video Embed
challenges.description: Create a responsive video embed container using aspect-ratio. The video should fill its container's width and maintain a 16:9 aspect ratio. No wrapper elements or padding hacks allowed.
challenges.hint: Set width: 100% and aspect-ratio: 16/9 directly on the video or iframe element.
challenges.expected_outcome: An iframe that fills the container width and maintains 16:9 proportion at any viewport size.

challenges.title: E-Commerce Product Grid
challenges.description: Build a responsive product grid where all product images maintain a consistent 4:3 aspect ratio. Use CSS Grid, aspect-ratio, and object-fit to create a uniform grid regardless of the original image dimensions. The grid should have 3 columns on desktop and 2 on mobile.
challenges.hint: Use grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr)) for responsive columns. Apply aspect-ratio: 4/3 and object-fit: cover on all images.

challenges.expected_outcome: A responsive product grid with 6 cards. All product image areas maintain consistent 4:3 ratio regardless of content, making the grid look uniform and professional.

challenges.title: Responsive Hero Section with Overlay Text
challenges.description: Build a hero section that uses aspect-ratio to maintain a proportional background area. The hero should have a background image/gradient with a specific aspect ratio on desktop (21:9) that transitions to a different ratio on mobile (4:3). Overlay text should remain readable and positioned using logical properties. Include a fallback for browsers without aspect-ratio support.
challenges.hint: Use aspect-ratio with media queries for different breakpoints. For the fallback, use @supports not (aspect-ratio: ...) with the padding-bottom hack. Use position: absolute for overlay text with inset-inline-start/end for logical positioning.

solution.expected_outcome: A hero section that maintains 21:9 ratio on desktop (cinematic look) and switches to 4:3 on mobile. Overlay text is centered. Falls back gracefully in browsers without aspect-ratio support.
